methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $13M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sarla Aviation is a Series A transportation company founded by Rakesh Gaonkar and Adrian Schmidt that has raised $13M to date. The transportation sector peer universe includes 8 private companies, notably Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and FleetZero, and no public comparables.

Valuation analysis relies on the Stage-Progression method, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage progression. Using the $13M total raised as a proxy base, this method yields $26M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable: while 3 same-sector transportation peers exist, none meet the threshold of 3+ companies in same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier cohorts required for a reliable narrow-set median. A broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity.

The triangulated valuation range is $22M (low) to $30M (high), with a median of $26M. This estimate is derived from a single method and should be treated as preliminary pending access to sufficient stage-matched and tier-matched comparables within the transportation sector. Key uncertainties include the lack of direct peer benchmarking and reliance on a generalized stage-progression multiplier, which may not capture sector-specific dynamics or the company's particular traction and market position.
